message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- optimize: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions. Target VPU, toolchain, build flags, FPGA board, and hardware profile all come from config.toml — no hardcoded paths or CPU names. Runs a 7-step FSM — validate, select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, learn — then loops back to analyze with new insights. No human intervention required.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	---
2	type: index
3	---
4	
5	# libnn Optimization Wiki
6	
7	Reference for the `/optimize` skill. Read this file first in `select` to identify relevant pages, then read the linked pages.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## General Techniques
12	
13	### GEMM Strategy
14	
15	- [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] -- Replace ENA_TILING GEMM (vectorize N) with im2col + vd4dots GEMM (vectorize K). Applies to all conv_HWC_s8 sym variants. 4-5x speedup. **Start here for any conv_HWC function.**
16	- [[vectorize-k-vs-n]] -- When to vectorize K vs N: K wins when K >> VL. N wins when N >= VLMAX_e8_m4/4. Includes work-per-instruction analysis.
17	- [[im2col-strategies]] -- Efficient im2col: 5-region bulk copy (center region), pad=0 fast path for dilated, 4-pixel batching. Applied after GEMM kernel is correct.
18	- [[runtime-dispatch]] -- VLMAX-based threshold to select kernel at runtime. Avoids regressions when N is large.
19	
20	### Requantization
21	
22	- [[vmulh-requantization]] -- Use vmulh (multiply-high, stays at e32) instead of vwmul (widens to e64) for requantization when shift >= 32. Avoids M8-to-M4 split. 1.06x on depthwise conv.
23	
24	### Accumulation and SEW
25	
26	- [[e16-accumulation]] -- Use e16/M4 instead of e32/M2 when values fit in int16. Doubles VL for pooling accumulators. Applied to avepool.
27	- [[lmul-selection]] -- LMUL/SEW reference table. Principle: match VL to the vectorized dimension. M1 for vd4dots register blocking.
28	
29	### Pipeline and Throughput
30	
31	- [[ax45mpv-vpu-uarch-spec]] -- **Data-sheet reference**: VPU functional units, VRF port assignments (read/write conflict tables), dual-issue restrictions, vector chaining rules, and exact latency/throughput formulas at VLEN=1024 DLEN=512. Read this when reasoning about pipeline stalls.
32	- [[ipc-vs-throughput]] -- IPC is misleading for vector kernels. Lower IPC + more work/instruction = faster. Always compare cycle counts.
33	
34	### Memory-Bound Analysis
35	
36	- [[memory-bound-diagnosis]] -- Symptoms (IPC ~0.30, neutral LMUL changes), diagnostic procedure, when to stop optimizing.
37	
38	### Anti-Patterns
39	
40	- [[anti-vd4dots-small-k]] -- Do NOT apply vd4dots when K < 32 (e.g., RGB conv with K=27). Direct conv wins.
41	- [[anti-lmul-reduction-memory-bound]] -- Increasing LMUL/parallelism does not help memory-bound functions. IPC improves but cycles stay flat.
42	- [[anti-sw-pipeline-same-reg-group]] -- Software pipelining fails when m8 has already used all register groups; the next-iter load reuses the same group the prev-iter store is draining. Pure code bloat.
43	
44	---
45	
46	## CPU Architecture Reference
47	
48	Data-sheet facts for the AX45MPV target (VLEN=1024, DLEN=512).
49	Read before proposing any pipeline-level optimization hypothesis.
50	
51	- [[ax45mpv-vpu-uarch-spec]] -- Complete VPU micro-architecture: 11 functional units,
52	  VRF read/write port conflict tables, dual-issue restrictions (8 rules), vector
53	  chaining mechanics, and concrete throughput/latency numbers for VALU / VMAC /
54	  vd4dots / vqmacc / VLSU from the data sheet (DS 1.5.2, 2.4.8, 23.15).
55	
56	---
57	
58	## Operator Profiles
59	
60	| Operator | Speedup | Key Technique | Status |
61	|---|---|---|---|
62	| [[conv-hwc-s8-sym-bias-fast]] | 4.35x | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] | Optimized 2026-03-23 |
63	| [[conv-hwc-s8-asym-bias-any]] | 4.97x | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] + [[im2col-strategies]] | Optimized 2026-03-24 |
64	| [[conv-1x1-asym-bias-fast-any]] | 2.60x | [[vectorize-k-vs-n]] + [[runtime-dispatch]] | Optimized 2026-03-25 |
65	| [[conv-hwc-rgb-sym-bias-fast]] | 2.57x | Direct conv ([[anti-vd4dots-small-k]]) | Optimized 2026-03-23 |
66	| [[conv-hwc-rgb-sft-bias-fast]] | 2.31x (cifar10) | VQMACC direct conv ([[anti-vd4dots-small-k]]) | Optimized 2026-04-13 |
67	| [[conv-hwc-s8-sft-bias-fast]] | 1.09x conv2, 1.05x conv3 (cifar10) | 4-pixel batch [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] | Optimized 2026-04-13 |
68	| [[avepool-hwc-s8]] | 2.78x | [[e16-accumulation]] | Optimized 2026-03-23 |
69	| [[conv-hwc-asym-bias-any-dilated]] | 1.30x | [[im2col-strategies]] pad=0 | Optimized 2026-03-24 |
70	| [[maxpool-hwc-s8]] | ~1.0x | Memory-bound -- no further opportunity | Exhausted 2026-03-23 |
71	| [[fc-mat-vec-s16-s16-s8-sft-bias-fast]] | ~1.0x (gru) | Weight layout locks algorithm; N-vectorize blocked | Algorithm exhausted 2026-04-14 |
72	| [[fc-s8-s8-s8-sft-bias-fast]] | 1.107x (kws-dnn), 1.16x IP2/IP3 | 2-row-group outer batching (shared input load) | Round 1 improved 2026-04-14 |
73	| [[conv-dw-hwc-u8-u8-u8-asym-bias-any]] | 1.06x | [[vmulh-requantization]] | Optimized 2026-04-17 |
74	| [[relu-s8]] | 1.00x (round 1 regressed) | Memory-bandwidth-bound at 43.9 B/cyc. SW-pipeline failed ([[anti-sw-pipeline-same-reg-group]]). | In-progress 2026-04-24 |
75	
76	---
77	
78	## Quick Lookup: Which Page to Read for a New Function
79	
80	| Function pattern | Read first |
81	|---|---|
82	| `conv_HWC_*_sym_*` | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]], then operator page |
83	| `conv_HWC_*_asym_*` | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]], [[im2col-strategies]], operator page |
84	| `conv_HWC_*_sft_*` | [[conv-hwc-s8-sft-bias-fast]] (4-pixel batch with unroll4) |
85	| `conv_1x1_*` | [[vectorize-k-vs-n]], [[runtime-dispatch]], [[conv-1x1-asym-bias-fast-any]] |
86	| `conv_HWC_*_RGB_*` | [[anti-vd4dots-small-k]], [[conv-hwc-rgb-sym-bias-fast]], [[conv-hwc-rgb-sft-bias-fast]] |
87	| `avepool_*` | [[e16-accumulation]], [[avepool-hwc-s8]] |
88	| `maxpool_*` | [[memory-bound-diagnosis]], [[maxpool-hwc-s8]] |
89	| `conv_dw_HWC_*` | [[vmulh-requantization]], [[conv-dw-hwc-u8-u8-u8-asym-bias-any]] |
90	| Any conv with dilation | [[im2col-strategies]] (pad=0 path), [[conv-hwc-asym-bias-any-dilated]] |

file.content:
---
type: index
---

# libnn Optimization Wiki

Reference for the `/optimize` skill. Read this file first in `select` to identify relevant pages, then read the linked pages.

---

## General Techniques

### GEMM Strategy

- [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] -- Replace ENA_TILING GEMM (vectorize N) with im2col + vd4dots GEMM (vectorize K). Applies to all conv_HWC_s8 sym variants. 4-5x speedup. **Start here for any conv_HWC function.**
- [[vectorize-k-vs-n]] -- When to vectorize K vs N: K wins when K >> VL. N wins when N >= VLMAX_e8_m4/4. Includes work-per-instruction analysis.
- [[im2col-strategies]] -- Efficient im2col: 5-region bulk copy (center region), pad=0 fast path for dilated, 4-pixel batching. Applied after GEMM kernel is correct.
- [[runtime-dispatch]] -- VLMAX-based threshold to select kernel at runtime. Avoids regressions when N is large.

### Requantization

- [[vmulh-requantization]] -- Use vmulh (multiply-high, stays at e32) instead of vwmul (widens to e64) for requantization when shift >= 32. Avoids M8-to-M4 split. 1.06x on depthwise conv.

### Accumulation and SEW

- [[e16-accumulation]] -- Use e16/M4 instead of e32/M2 when values fit in int16. Doubles VL for pooling accumulators. Applied to avepool.
- [[lmul-selection]] -- LMUL/SEW reference table. Principle: match VL to the vectorized dimension. M1 for vd4dots register blocking.

### Pipeline and Throughput

- [[ax45mpv-vpu-uarch-spec]] -- **Data-sheet reference**: VPU functional units, VRF port assignments (read/write conflict tables), dual-issue restrictions, vector chaining rules, and exact latency/throughput formulas at VLEN=1024 DLEN=512. Read this when reasoning about pipeline stalls.
- [[ipc-vs-throughput]] -- IPC is misleading for vector kernels. Lower IPC + more work/instruction = faster. Always compare cycle counts.

### Memory-Bound Analysis

- [[memory-bound-diagnosis]] -- Symptoms (IPC ~0.30, neutral LMUL changes), diagnostic procedure, when to stop optimizing.

### Anti-Patterns

- [[anti-vd4dots-small-k]] -- Do NOT apply vd4dots when K < 32 (e.g., RGB conv with K=27). Direct conv wins.
- [[anti-lmul-reduction-memory-bound]] -- Increasing LMUL/parallelism does not help memory-bound functions. IPC improves but cycles stay flat.
- [[anti-sw-pipeline-same-reg-group]] -- Software pipelining fails when m8 has already used all register groups; the next-iter load reuses the same group the prev-iter store is draining. Pure code bloat.

---

## CPU Architecture Reference

Data-sheet facts for the AX45MPV target (VLEN=1024, DLEN=512).
Read before proposing any pipeline-level optimization hypothesis.

- [[ax45mpv-vpu-uarch-spec]] -- Complete VPU micro-architecture: 11 functional units,
  VRF read/write port conflict tables, dual-issue restrictions (8 rules), vector
  chaining mechanics, and concrete throughput/latency numbers for VALU / VMAC /
  vd4dots / vqmacc / VLSU from the data sheet (DS 1.5.2, 2.4.8, 23.15).

---

## Operator Profiles

| Operator | Speedup | Key Technique | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| [[conv-hwc-s8-sym-bias-fast]] | 4.35x | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] | Optimized 2026-03-23 |
| [[conv-hwc-s8-asym-bias-any]] | 4.97x | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] + [[im2col-strategies]] | Optimized 2026-03-24 |
| [[conv-1x1-asym-bias-fast-any]] | 2.60x | [[vectorize-k-vs-n]] + [[runtime-dispatch]] | Optimized 2026-03-25 |
| [[conv-hwc-rgb-sym-bias-fast]] | 2.57x | Direct conv ([[anti-vd4dots-small-k]]) | Optimized 2026-03-23 |
| [[conv-hwc-rgb-sft-bias-fast]] | 2.31x (cifar10) | VQMACC direct conv ([[anti-vd4dots-small-k]]) | Optimized 2026-04-13 |
| [[conv-hwc-s8-sft-bias-fast]] | 1.09x conv2, 1.05x conv3 (cifar10) | 4-pixel batch [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]] | Optimized 2026-04-13 |
| [[avepool-hwc-s8]] | 2.78x | [[e16-accumulation]] | Optimized 2026-03-23 |
| [[conv-hwc-asym-bias-any-dilated]] | 1.30x | [[im2col-strategies]] pad=0 | Optimized 2026-03-24 |
| [[maxpool-hwc-s8]] | ~1.0x | Memory-bound -- no further opportunity | Exhausted 2026-03-23 |
| [[fc-mat-vec-s16-s16-s8-sft-bias-fast]] | ~1.0x (gru) | Weight layout locks algorithm; N-vectorize blocked | Algorithm exhausted 2026-04-14 |
| [[fc-s8-s8-s8-sft-bias-fast]] | 1.107x (kws-dnn), 1.16x IP2/IP3 | 2-row-group outer batching (shared input load) | Round 1 improved 2026-04-14 |
| [[conv-dw-hwc-u8-u8-u8-asym-bias-any]] | 1.06x | [[vmulh-requantization]] | Optimized 2026-04-17 |
| [[relu-s8]] | 1.00x (round 1 regressed) | Memory-bandwidth-bound at 43.9 B/cyc. SW-pipeline failed ([[anti-sw-pipeline-same-reg-group]]). | In-progress 2026-04-24 |

---

## Quick Lookup: Which Page to Read for a New Function

| Function pattern | Read first |
|---|---|
| `conv_HWC_*_sym_*` | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]], then operator page |
| `conv_HWC_*_asym_*` | [[tiling-gemm-to-vd4dots]], [[im2col-strategies]], operator page |
| `conv_HWC_*_sft_*` | [[conv-hwc-s8-sft-bias-fast]] (4-pixel batch with unroll4) |
| `conv_1x1_*` | [[vectorize-k-vs-n]], [[runtime-dispatch]], [[conv-1x1-asym-bias-fast-any]] |
| `conv_HWC_*_RGB_*` | [[anti-vd4dots-small-k]], [[conv-hwc-rgb-sym-bias-fast]], [[conv-hwc-rgb-sft-bias-fast]] |
| `avepool_*` | [[e16-accumulation]], [[avepool-hwc-s8]] |
| `maxpool_*` | [[memory-bound-diagnosis]], [[maxpool-hwc-s8]] |
| `conv_dw_HWC_*` | [[vmulh-requantization]], [[conv-dw-hwc-u8-u8-u8-asym-bias-any]] |
| Any conv with dilation | [[im2col-strategies]] (pad=0 path), [[conv-hwc-asym-bias-any-dilated]] |
